key_insight: Higher cold-spray pressure drives copper particles faster past the bonding threshold, producing denser, more ductile deposits with better conductivity but lower hardness.
engineering_relevance: For CSAM copper parts, tuning spray pressure is a practical lever to balance electrical performance, ductility, and structural integrity in one process window.
method: Cold-sprayed copper at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar using a LightSPEE3D system
Measured particle/material response with XRD, SEM, hardness, conductivity, and tensile tests
Compared microstructure and properties against spray pressure to identify the optimum window
evidence_signal: The abstract reports higher-pressure deposits showed refined crystallites, increased conductivity, improved ductility, and fully ductile fracture, while hardness decreased with recovery/recrystallisation.
watch_item: The optimum pressure window is system-specific, so transfer to other nozzles, powders, or substrates should be validated before implementation.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
